result:
Reports written to:
- `reports/2026-02-23-security-review.md`
- `reports/2026-02-23-security-review.json`

**Summary: 10 findings — 1 high, 2 medium, 3 low, 4 info**

| # | Severity | Finding |
|---|----------|---------|
| 1 | HIGH | `GoogleService-Info.plist` (Firebase API key + GCM sender ID) committed to git |
| 2 | MEDIUM | `lib/meta/api.ts` (Meta Graph API server code) bundled inside mobile app — no leak today but architectural risk |
| 3 | MEDIUM | `EXPO_PUBLIC_SCREENSHOT_MODE=1` bypasses all StoreKit purchase verification; a production build with this flag grants free premium to everyone |
| 4 | LOW | Facebook App ID hardcoded in `app.config.ts` instead of env var |
| 5 | LOW | `activityDates` and `completedLessonIds` arrays grow unbounded — could cause AsyncStorage write failures over time |
| 6 | LOW | No certificate pinning on external API calls |
| 7–10 | INFO | Unvalidated `track/` deep link param, untyped store migration cast, plain-AsyncStorage feedback keys, EAS project ID in source |

**Positive findings:** Purchase state is never persisted to AsyncStorage (verified via `partialize`), premium is verified against Apple StoreKit on every launch, Tier 4 content is double-gated at the router and lesson screen, deep link lesson IDs are validated against the content manifest, analytics collects no PII and omits stack traces.

REVIEW_COMPLETE
